fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer pharmacist license to new hampshire, pharmacist compact new hampshire, new hampshire pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: New Hampshire Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)
board.mailing_address: OPLC - Board of Pharmacy, 7 Eagle Square, Concord, NH 03301

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Create OPLC Online Licensing System Account
steps.description: Register for a new account or log in to your existing profile on the OPLC Online Licensing System at nh.virtreg.com.

steps.title: Initiate Endorsement Application
steps.description: From your OPLC Online Licensing System dashboard, select and begin the 'Pharmacist - Endorsement' application.

steps.title: Complete Application Sections
steps.description: Accurately fill out all required personal, professional, and disciplinary history information within the online application forms.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit all necessary supporting documents, including official transcripts, license verifications from other states, and NABP e-Passport (for MPJE & NAPLEX scores), as specified in the application checklist.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable endorsement application fee of $200.00 through the OPLC Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within the OPLC Online Licensing System and promptly respond to any requests for additional information from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in New Hampshire. To work in New Hampshire, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in New Hampshire
hero.dek: Pharmacists seeking licensure in New Hampshire must apply through the OPLC Online Licensing System, which is managed by the Office of Professional Licensure and Certification.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the OPLC Online Licensing System, click 'Register an Account' to create a new user profile, and then log in to access the application options.
